##NAMES=Romeite
##RRUFFID=R060323
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(Ca,Fe^2+^,Mn^2+^,Na)_2_(Sb^5+^,Ti^4+^)_2_O_6_(O,OH,F)
##LOCALITY=Praborna mine, Aosta Valley, St. Marcel, Italy
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Yellowish-brown octahedra, associated with red piemontite, R060905. Note that this is a fluorine rich phase.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R060323
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=dark: (Ca_1.44_[box]_0.25_Na_0.24_Fe^2+^_0.05_Mn^2+^_0.02_)_Σ=2_(Sb^5+^_1.74_Ti^4+^_0.26_)_Σ=2_O_6_(F_0.65_(OH)_0.35_)_Σ=1_ ; light: (Ca_1.33_[box]_0.32_Na_0.26_Fe^2+^_0.06_Mn^2+^_0.03_)_Σ=2_(Sb^5+^_1.90_Ti^4+^_0.10_)_Σ=2_O_6_(F_0.56_(OH)_0.44_)_Σ=1
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.273 b: 10.273 c: 10.273 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1084.1 crystal system: cubic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
